name: Address every open tension recorded in data/
requirement: Any candidate framework must give a coherent account of every entry in data/ whose status is 'open' or 'contested'. 'Coherent account' means: either (a) reproducing the standard prediction so that the tension is shifted to a measurement systematic, (b) predicting the observed value from genuinely new physics with no additional fine-tuning, or (c) explicitly identifying which entry the framework treats as a measurement systematic and which it treats as new physics, and accepting the consequences of that choice elsewhere in the catalog (the 'cost_elsewhere' fields).
what_it_excludes: A framework that explains one tension while violating others is not progress, it is bookkeeping. This benchmark forces candidate frameworks to face the cross-tension landscape. In particular: any framework that fixes H_0 must say what happens to S_8; any framework that explains a CDF-style m_W must say what happens to ATLAS and CMS; any framework that predicts a large muon-dipole contribution must accommodate the lattice-vs-data-driven HVP situation.
notes: This entry is meta - it points back into data/. The list under tension_links should be kept in sync as data/ grows. A framework does not have to predict every tension to be interesting, but it must be explicit about which it is solving and which it is leaving alone.

primary_references.citation: See data/ entries for primary literature on each tension.
